response: Bat, the amateur historian can calculate the number of average purchases by first determining the total value of the tokens issued. Each token is worth 3 pence, and the village issued 240 tokens, so the total value is 240 tokens × 3 pence/token = 720 pence. If the average purchase cost 18 pence, the number of purchases that could be made is 720 pence ÷ 18 pence/purchase = 40 purchases. Therefore, with the total number of tokens issued, the village could make 40 average purchases. P.P.S.: lake
